name: chart-generator
description: Generate charts and visualizations from data using various charting libraries and formats.
capabilities: Create bar, line, pie, scatter, and histogram charts
Export charts as PNG, SVG, PDF, and HTML
Apply professional color themes and styling
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing example code snippets for chart generation using legitimate data visualization libraries (matplotlib, seaborn, plotly, chartjs). All static findings are false positives: markdown code block markers were misidentified as shell backticks, normal documentation text triggered false cryptographic/reconnaissance patterns, and no actual network calls or credential access exist. The skill provides templates for creating static and interactive charts.
valueStatement: Transform raw data into compelling visual stories. Create bar charts, line graphs, scatter plots, heatmaps, and interactive dashboards with professional styling.
limitations: Interactive charts require HTML export for full functionality

useCases.title: Business Report Visualizations
useCases.description: Create sales trends, performance metrics, and KPI dashboards for executive presentations

useCases.title: Scientific Data Visualization
useCases.description: Generate publication-ready charts for research papers and statistical analysis

useCases.title: Application Analytics
useCases.description: Build interactive charts for web applications and user analytics dashboards

promptTemplates.title: Basic Chart Creation
promptTemplates.scenario: Create a simple bar chart from CSV data
promptTemplates.prompt:
Generate a bar chart showing sales by product category from this data: Category, Sales
Electronics, 45000
Clothing, 32000
Books, 18000
Home, 28000

promptTemplates.title: Time Series Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a line chart showing monthly revenue trends for 2024 with proper labels and grid lines

promptTemplates.title: Statistical Visualization
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a histogram with normal distribution overlay and a correlation heatmap for this dataset

promptTemplates.title: Interactive Dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an interactive HTML dashboard with bar chart, line chart, pie chart, and scatter plot using Plotly

bestPractices: Choose chart types that match your data story and audience needs
Use consistent color schemes and clear labels for better readability
Export charts in vector formats (SVG, PDF) for print publications
antiPatterns: Avoid 3D charts that can distort data perception and readability
Don't use pie charts for comparing more than 5-7 categories
Never skip axis labels and units - they provide essential context

faq.question: Which chart type should I use for time series data?
faq.answer: Use line charts for continuous time series, area charts for cumulative data, and bar charts for discrete time periods.

faq.question: Can I customize colors and themes?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides multiple color palettes including corporate, pastel, vibrant, and monochrome themes.

faq.question: How do I create interactive charts for web?
faq.answer: Use Plotly examples to generate HTML files with zoom, pan, and hover interactions that work in any browser.

faq.question: What's the best format for print publications?
faq.answer: Export as PDF or SVG for vector graphics that scale without quality loss, or use PNG at 300+ DPI for raster images.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple chart types?
faq.answer: Yes, use subplot layouts or dashboard templates to combine different chart types in a single visualization.

faq.question: How do I handle large datasets?
faq.answer: Consider sampling, aggregation, or using interactive libraries like Plotly that handle large data efficiently.
